name: Sefirot (Divine Emanations)
shortDescription: The Sefirot are the ten emanations of God in Kabbalah, representing different aspects of the divine and serving as a framework for understanding the universe.
description: The Sefirot (סְפִירוֹת), meaning 'enumerations,' are the ten attributes or emanations in Kabbalah, through which the Ein Sof (the Infinite) reveals itself and continuously creates both the physical realm and the chain of higher metaphysical realms (Seder hishtalshelut). They are not simply attributes of God, but rather dynamic and interconnected forces that shape reality. Each Sefirah represents a different aspect of the divine, ranging from Keter (Crown), representing divine will and origin, to Malkhut (Kingdom), representing the physical world and the divine presence within it. In between are Hochmah (Wisdom), Binah (Understanding), Hesed (Loving-Kindness), Gevurah (Severity/Judgment), Tiferet (Beauty/Harmony), Netzach (Victory/Endurance), Hod (Splendor/Majesty), and Yesod (Foundation/Bond). The Sefirot are often visualized as a tree, the Etz Chaim (Tree of Life), with interconnected pathways illustrating the flow of divine energy. The arrangement and interrelationship of the Sefirot are crucial for maintaining balance and harmony in the cosmos. Disruption in the flow of energy between them can lead to imbalance and chaos. They serve as a model for understanding the structure of the universe, the human soul, and the relationship between God and creation. The concept of Sefirot is central to Kabbalistic thought and practice, influencing Jewish mysticism, ethics, and ritual. The menorah can be seen as a symbolic representation of the sefirot.
